text: Risk management is an essential process for any successful project or business. It begins with identifying potential risks, such as financial problems, delays, or technical issues. Once risks are identified, teams can develop mitigation strategies to reduce their impact or likelihood. For example, a company might hire extra staff or order materials early to avoid delays. However, not all risks can be prevented, so contingency plans are also important. These are backup actions, like using a different supplier, if a problem occurs. Finally, teams must continuously monitor risks and plans, because situations can change. Regular check-ins allow for adjustments, keeping the project on track and minimising surprises.
